mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with falling FEDFUNDS across 10-year out-of-sample windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force equity sell-offs and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the rapid unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in real sales of final goods and housing starts confirm synchronized demand expansion across consumer and capital goods sectors, driving earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns in > 70% of historical occurrences, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and forcing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not predict negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant or declining federal funds rate creates a negative real interest rate environment that triggers central bank intervention fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM while FEDFUNDS falls or stays flat
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and flat/falling FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained labor market strength and rising aggregate consumption, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP exceeding 0.5% signals immediate demand contraction and recession onset, triggering rapid portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 prints fail to precede negative 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of historical instances, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and declining unemployment confirms a healthy supply-demand balance without inflationary pressure, supporting stable corporate margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 70% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.
